holdings.infoBox: UAE is an equity ETF with a total of 168 individual holdings. The top holdings are First Abu Dhabi Bank P.J.S.C. stock at 12.29%, EMAAR PROPERTIES at 11.62%, Emirates Telecommunications Group Company PJSC at 10.44%, EMIRATES NBD at 8.51%, and AED CASH at 4.62%.

overview.description: The iShares MSCI UAE ETF (UAE) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in total market equity. The fund tracks a market-cap-weighted index of large-, mid and small-cap UAE companies. UAE was launched on Apr 29, 2014 and is issued by BlackRock.

data.title: UAE's Fujairah marine bunker sales hit record low in May
data.text: Marine fuel sales at the United Arab Emirates' Fujairah ​port extended declines in May to ‌hit a record low, latest data showed, as cargo replenishment stayed constrained after ​the Middle East war cr...

data.title: UAE denies 'false' reports of fund transfer to Iran
data.text: The United Arab Emirates denies media reports alleging the government has agreed to release up to $20 billion in frozen assets to Iran. The UAE says 'no frozen Iranian funds have been released'.

data.title: Medicus Pharma Initiates Groundbreaking PRECISION-E2 Phase 2a Study of Teverelix® in Endometriosis utilizing the Emirati Genome Program
data.text: Novel genomics-enabled clinical trial in United Arab Emirates (UAE) seeks to identify genetic predictors of treatment response while evaluating Teverelix® as a potential long-acting precision medicine...

data.title: The UAE's Exit From OPEC
data.text: On May 1, the United Arab Emirates (UAE) formally exited both the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC) and the broader OPEC+ grouping of major oil producers. Such exits are not unh...

data.title: UAE says its decision to leave OPEC was a strategic economic move, not a political one
data.text: The UAE says its decision to leave OPEC and OPEC+ was based on the country's national interest. Energy Minister Suhail Mohamed Al Mazrouei says it remains committed to maintaining market stability.

data.title: UAE says OPEC, OPEC+ exit was sovereign strategic decision, not political move
data.text: The United ​Arab Emirates' decision ‌to withdraw from OPEC ​and OPEC+ ​was a sovereign ⁠and strategic ​choice based ​on a comprehensive assessment of its ​production policy ​and future capabilities, ‌...

data.title: UAE says it is discussing currency swap line with US
data.text: The United Arab Emirates is discussing a currency swap line with the United States, ​its trade minister said on Monday.

data.title: UAE's exit from OPEC not directed against anyone, ADNOC CEO says
data.text: The United Arab Emirates' decision to exit OPEC and OPEC+ was not directed against ​anyone but meant to serve national interests, Abu Dhabi National ‌Oil Company CEO Sultan Al Jaber said on Monday.

data.title: UAE could draw higher US investment on pumping more oil, J.P. Morgan says
data.text: J.P. Morgan on Thursday said the UAE could attract greater investments from U.S. ​companies once it is in a position ‌to pump more oil after the country decided to leave the Organization of Petroleum ...

data.title: CNBC Daily Open: UAE leaves OPEC adrift
data.text: The United Arab Emirates will exit OPEC on May 1. European bank earnings top forecasts, but Airbus misses expectations.

data.title: UAE Energy Minister explains decision to leave OPEC as Hormuz crisis deepens
data.text: Suhail Al Mazrouei, the UAE's Energy Minister speaks to CNBC's Dan Murphy following the country's announcement it will leave OPEC. The Minister described the move as a "pure policy change", dismissing...

data.title: UAE Leaving OPEC at Right Time, Energy Minister Says
data.text: The United Arab Emirates is leaving OPEC on May 1, dealing a blow to the group and its leader Saudi Arabia. UAE Energy Minister Suhail Al Mazrouei tells Bloomberg's Joumanna Bercetche that the timing ...

data.title: United Arab Emirates to leave OPEC on May 1 in shocking blow to world's largest oil cartel amid Iran war
data.text: Though the Middle Eastern nation did not give a reason for its exit, some of its oil infrastructure was recently damaged by drone strikes from fellow OPEC member Iran.
